name: Trackmasters
description:
The '''Trackmasters''', also known as '''Poke & Tone''', is an American [[Hip-hop production|hip hop production]] outfit composed of Poke (Jean-Claude Olivier born January 21, 1970; [[Brooklyn|Brooklyn, New York]] ) and Tone (Samuel Barnes born January 20, 1969; [[Brooklyn|Brooklyn, New York]]). Active from the mid-late 1980s to the early 2000s, the group was more often known as a duo, '''Poke & Tone'''. Throughout their career, they have been credited on albums and singles for hip hop and R&B artists including [[Destiny's Child]], [[Nas]], [[R. Kelly]], [[LL Cool J]], [[Mary J. Blige]], [[Will Smith]], [[Jay-Z]], [[Cam'ron]], [[Jennifer Lopez]], [[Mariah Carey]], [[The Notorious B.I.G.]] and [[50 Cent]].

Known for their commercially successful singles and remixes, they have collectively earned over seven gold and 20 platinum certifications by the [[Recording Industry Association of America]]. They were ranked 6th in [[Vibe (magazine)|''Vibe'']] magazine's "Greatest Hip-Hop Producers of All Time" in the 'Mass Appeal' category.
